reforms.description: The Australian government launched a review of the higher education sector, resulting in a A$1½ billion increase in support to universities over four years and the introduction of a new Commonwealth Grant Scheme (CGS) to replace traditional block operating grants. This reform aims to align funding with the economy's needs and improve the quality of higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and improved alignment with economic needs are expected to enhance the quality of higher education, thereby supporting long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes in the funding of higher education, impacting the entire sector.
reforms.source_quote: Important initiatives included: a A$1½ billion increase in support to universities over four years; changes in commonwealth funding for higher institutions; greater flexibility for universities in setting course fees.

reforms.description: The Australian Government launched the National Skills Shortages Strategy to address skill shortages in key industries, incorporating new approaches to apprenticeships and encouraging regional collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing skill shortages and enhancing workforce capabilities, this initiative is expected to improve productivity and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been executed and is actively addressing skill shortages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve skills in the workforce, although it is not as transformative as the higher education funding reform.
reforms.source_quote: In 2004, the government launched the National Skills Shortages Strategy to tackle skill shortages in key industries throughout the country.
